commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And he took the elders of the city, and thorns of the wilderness and briers, and with them he taught the men of Succoth. Hebrew seems to be corrupted in this place. "And he showed (instructed or chastised) with these thorns. "The Septuagint and Vulgate read the same word as ver. He crushed the people with such instruments as are used to beat out corn. It is probable that he only treated the magistrates of Soccoth and of Phanuel in this manner.
